title: Echioglossum seidenfadenii
taxonomy: Echioglossum seidenfadenii is classified as a species within the family Orobanchaceae, a family of flowering plants commonly known as broomrapes or stickyworts. It belongs to the order Lamiales, which comprises many flowering plant families including the mint, olive, and figwort families. The genus Echioglossum is a small one with only two species, both endemic to Southeast Asia. Echioglossum seidenfadenii is a holoparasitic plant, meaning it obtains all its nutrients from other plants. This plant has a unique growth habit, often forming a dense mat on the ground.
uses: Echioglossum seidenfadenii is a plant species found in Madagascar. Unfortunately, there is limited information available on the medicinal uses of this plant.
